_comment: Machine-readable print equivalents for all named brand colors used in tokens.css. Values transcribed from the Scouting America Brand Guidelines 2024 (May 2024 edition). CMYK and Pantone apply to offset printing on coated stock unless noted. Where the PDF does not supply a value the field is null with an explanation in _note. This file is emitted to dist/tokens.print.json by scripts/build-css.mjs.

colors.name: sa-blue
colors.label: Scouting America Blue
colors.source: p.9, p.14: mark reproduction and primary palette swatch

colors.name: sa-red
colors.label: Scouting America Red
colors.source: p.9, p.14: mark reproduction and primary palette swatch

colors.name: sa-dark-gray
colors.label: Scouting America Dark Gray
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-gray
colors.label: Scouting America Gray
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-pale-gray
colors.label: Scouting America Pale Gray
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-tan
colors.label: Scouting America Tan
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-light-tan
colors.label: Scouting America Light Tan
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-dark-tan
colors.label: Scouting America Dark Tan
colors._note: Secondary support color. No Pantone or CMYK listed in the 2024 guidelines; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-pale-blue
colors.label: Scouting America Pale Blue
colors._note: Secondary color listed on p.14. No Pantone or CMYK listed; convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: sa-dark-blue
colors.label: Scouting America Dark Blue
colors._note: Secondary color listed on p.14. No Pantone or CMYK listed for the standalone swatch; CMYK for the Sea Scouts mark is 100/72/27/33 (different print context). Convert hex with print vendor for parent brand use.

colors.name: cub-blue
colors.label: Cub Scouts Blue
colors.source: p.22: Cub Scouts mark reproduction. Note: the K value (44) differs from the parent SA Blue mark spec (K=21) on the same Pantone. Use K=44 for Cub Scouts materials.
colors._note: Same Pantone as SA Blue but a noticeably different CMYK formula. Both values are verbatim from the PDF; the 2024 guidelines list them separately.

colors.name: scoutsbsa-olive
colors.label: Scouts BSA Olive
colors._note: The 2024 guidelines list the hex and RGB on p.25 but no Pantone or CMYK for the olive swatch. The mark reproduction table (p.25) covers only the emblem colors (tan/gold, brown, blue, red), not the olive field. Convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: scoutsbsa-tan
colors.label: Scouts BSA Tan
colors._note: Listed on p.25 as a program color. No Pantone or CMYK in the guidelines; this swatch is shared with SA Tan. Convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: scoutsbsa-red
colors.label: Scouts BSA Red
colors.source: p.25: Scouts BSA mark reproduction (same spec as SA Red)
colors._note: This is the print accent for Scouts BSA marks. The design system uses SA Blue (#003F87) as the digital accent for Scouts BSA to reduce aggressive red in UI; the red is preserved here for print mark fidelity.

colors.name: scoutsbsa-emblem-brown
colors.label: Scouts BSA Emblem Brown
colors._note: Appears only in the BSA emblem mark. Not used as a UI token; included here for print-production completeness.

colors.name: seascouts-dark-blue
colors.label: Sea Scouts Dark Blue
colors.source: p.31: Sea Scouts mark reproduction (dark blue component)
colors._note: No Pantone is listed for Sea Scouts Dark Blue anywhere in the 2024 guidelines. The CMYK is from the mark reproduction table on p.31 where it appears as the 'Dark Blue' field.

colors.name: seascouts-yellow
colors.label: Sea Scouts Yellow
colors._note: No Pantone is listed for Sea Scouts Yellow in the 2024 guidelines. The mark reproduction table on p.31 contains a 'Dark Yellow' row with CMYK 0/25/100/100 and RGB 255/204/0 but the K=100 value contradicts a bright yellow at any press condition and is almost certainly a column-alignment artifact from the PDF text extraction. Do not use that CMYK for production. Convert hex with print vendor.

colors.name: seascouts-light-blue
colors.label: Sea Scouts Light Blue
colors.source: p.31: Sea Scouts mark reproduction (light blue component)
colors._note: No Pantone listed. CMYK taken from the mark reproduction table; treat as a starting point and verify with print vendor.

_discrepancies.item: SA Blue vs Cub Blue CMYK
_discrepancies.detail: Both colors share hex #003F87 and Pantone PMS 294, but the PDF lists different CMYK formulas: SA Blue mark = C100/M58/Y0/K21 (p.9) vs Cub Scouts mark = C100/M48/Y0/K44 (p.22). This is not an extraction error: the SA Red CMYK is identical on both pages (0/100/81/4), confirming the Cub Blue value is intentionally different. Use the program-specific formula for each brand context.

The PDF mark reproduction table on p.31 lists 'Dark Yellow: 0, 25, 100, 100' with screen RGB 255/204/0 (#FFCC00). A K=100 channel (pure black) alongside zero ink channels is physically impossible for a bright yellow. The 100 value for K is almost certainly a column shift in the extracted text; the actual value is unknown. CMYK is marked null; convert hex with vendor.

_discrepancies.item: Sea Scouts Dark Blue HEX typo in brand-text.txt
_discrepancies.detail: Page 32 of the brand text (line ~1039) shows 'Sea Scouts Dark Blue HEX: #FFCC00' but then RGB: 0, 51, 102 -- the hex label is a copy-paste error in the extracted text. The correct hex is #003366 as confirmed by the RGB triplet and the mark CMYK Dark Blue: 100/72/27/33 matching that value.
